question: What is the main gotcha about fetch behavior with HTTP 404 or 500?

options.text: fetch does NOT throw an error — the promise resolves successfully, you must check response.ok
fetch throws an HttpError with the status code
options.explanation: fetch doesn't throw on HTTP 4xx/5xx — this is the key difference from axios and other libraries

options.text: fetch returns null on server error
options.explanation: fetch returns a Response object even with error status codes

options.text: fetch automatically retries the request on 5xx
options.explanation: fetch has no built-in retry mechanism

question: Which situations cause the fetch promise to actually reject?

options.text: No network connection (TypeError)
DNS not found (TypeError)
Cancellation via AbortController (AbortError)
HTTP 404 Not Found
options.explanation: HTTP 404 doesn't reject the promise — response.ok will be false, but the promise resolves

options.text: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
options.explanation: HTTP 500 doesn't reject the promise — this is the gotcha behavior of the fetch API

question: Why shouldn't you retry HTTP 4xx errors?

options.text: 4xx are client errors, retrying won't change anything
Retrying 4xx causes a compilation error
options.explanation: Retrying 4xx is technically possible but pointless — the server will respond the same way

options.text: The server will block the client on repeated 4xx requests
options.explanation: Blocking is a side effect, not the main reason. The key point is the request is guaranteed to fail

options.text: 4xx errors can't be caught by catch
options.explanation: 4xx errors can be caught if you check response.ok and throw an error

question: Why is AbortController needed when fetching data in useEffect?

options.text: To cancel the request when the component unmounts, preventing state updates on unmounted components
To speed up data loading
options.explanation: AbortController doesn't speed up loading — it cancels unnecessary requests

options.text: For automatic retry on error
options.explanation: AbortController cancels requests, it doesn't retry them

options.text: React doesn't work without AbortController
options.explanation: React works without AbortController, but without it there can be memory leaks and errors

question: What should you do with an AbortError when using AbortController?

options.text: Filter and ignore it — it's not a real error, but an expected cancellation
Show the user an error message
options.explanation: AbortError on unmount is expected behavior, the user doesn't need to see it

options.text: Log it to a monitoring service
options.explanation: AbortError is a normal situation during navigation, it doesn't require monitoring

options.text: Automatically retry the request
options.explanation: If the component is unmounted, retrying is pointless and will cause a leak
